data.title: Indian shares fall as U.S. tariff worries persist
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes extended losses on Friday as investors fretted over the implications of Trump's plans to impose reciprocal tariffs, which analysts said could hurt the country the most among its Asian peers.

U.S. President Donald Trump is planning to slap reciprocal tariffs on every country taxing U.S. imports. The imposition of these duties, however, is likely to be delayed, helping global stocks stage a relief rally.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met Trump on Thursday and offered to talk about easing tariffs, buying more U.S. oil and gas, combat aircraft and concessions.

But that did little to calm markets back home on worries that India could be hit hard by reciprocal tariffs. The Nifty 50 and the BSE Sensex lost about 0.8% each as of 2:25 p.m. IST.

"Indian markets have seen sharper losses on the day due to the high tariff differential with the U.S., compared to most other Asian economies," said Narendra Solanki, head of research at Anand Rathi.

Analysts said the potential consequences of U.S. tariffs on the Indian rupee and U.S. interest rates could trigger further foreign outflows, hurting domestic equities.

All 13 major sectors were down, with drug makers , which have significant revenue exposure to the U.S., falling the most with a 3.41% decline.

Telecom operator Vodafone Idea fell 5.6% and Bharti Airtel was down 0.1% after India's top court dismissed a set of review petitions filed to overturn the earlier order on the calculation of dues.

The smallcaps and midcaps tumbled 4% and 3%, respectively, and continued their downward trend on fears of slowing corporate earnings and stretched valuations.

The smallcap index is currently down about 21% from its record in December, while midcaps are 19.5% off from their peak closing level September.

data.title: Singapore Shares Edge 0.1% Lower; Banks End Mixed — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index closed 0.1% lower at 3877.50, following two consecutive days of gains. Markets are likely digesting the changing U.S. tariff policies as well as the hotter-than-expected inflation data this week. Investors will be eyeing the U.S. retail sales numbers due later today for further clues on the health of the U.S. economy, the Commerzbank Research team wrote in a note. Among the biggest decliners, Singapore Exchange fell 5.8%, DFI Retail Group lost 2.6% and Singtel was 1.5% lower. Banks were mixed, with UOB and OCBC up 1.1% and 0.3%, respectively, while DBS Group shed 0.5%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Procore Technologies Is Maintained at Overweight by Piper Sandler
data.description: (09:33 GMT) Procore Technologies Price Target Raised to $110.00/Share From $90.00 by Piper Sandler

data.title: Emirates Reem Investments Halts Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Emirates Reem Investments (DFM:ERC) shares paused trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 9:30 am local time on Friday due to a board meeting.

The Dubai-based bottled mineral water company will resume trading after it publishes its meeting results, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Airbus Unit Selects Patria for Drone Communication System Datalink in Spain
data.description:
Airbus division Airbus Defence and Space signed an agreement with Patria in Spain for a datalink system for aerial vehicles and ground system communication.

The defense and technology company will deploy a wide-band datalink technology based on its Patria CANDL products for SIRTAP, a high-capability tactical unmanned aircraft system, according to a Friday release.

Patria is 50.1% owned by the state of Finland, with Norway-based Kongsberg Gruppen's unit Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace holding the remaining 49.9%.r

data.title: Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank to Halt Trading in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank (ADX:ADIB) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 9 am local time on March 10 due to its annual general meeting.

The Abu Dhabi-based lender will resume trading after it publishes the results of the AGM, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Lagardere and its majority owner Louis Hachette jump as FY results rise
data.description:
** French media company Lagardere rise more than 9% on FY results and a dividend proposal of EUR 0.67 per share

** Lagardere publishes FY EBIT of 593 million euros ($621.52 million), up 14% year-on-year, on revenues up 8.5% LFL to about 9 billion euros

** The shares is on track for its best day since mid-September 2021, if gains hold

** They touch their highest price in 4-1/2 months

** Shares of Louis Hachette Group , majority owner of Lagardere, also climb 9.3% after announcing plans to distribute at least 85% of the dividends received from Lagardere in 2025

** Vivendi , from which Louis Hachette Group was spun-off, edges up 1.9%

($1 = 0.9541 euros)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0933 GMT - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index closed 0.1% lower at 3877.50, following two consecutive days of gains. Markets are likely digesting the changing U.S. tariff policies as well as the hotter-than-expected inflation data this week. Investors will be eyeing the U.S. retail sales numbers due later today for further clues on the health of the U.S. economy, the Commerzbank Research team wrote in a note. Among the biggest decliners, Singapore Exchange fell 5.8%, DFI Retail Group lost 2.6% and Singtel was 1.5% lower. Banks were mixed, with UOB and OCBC up 1.1% and 0.3%, respectively, while DBS Group shed 0.5%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0932 GMT - NatWest's profitability guidance could be unsustainable, Shore Capital analyst Gary Greenwood writes in a research note. The U.K. lender guided for its return on tangible equity--a key measure of profitability--to land in the 15% to 16% range in 2025, and to stay over 15% in 2027. This represents an uplift from its previous guidanceand aligns with consensus expectations.. "While a RoTE of this magnitude may be achievable in the near-term, we worry whether it will being sustainable over the longer-term given competitive pressure and the risk that such a return could attract the wrong sort of political and regulatory attention," Greenwood notes. Shares trade 1.7% lower at 429.4 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0914 GMT - Umicore's 2024 results and guidance for the year ahead are in line with expectations, but this came alongside a cut and reset to its dividend, ING's Stijn Demeester says in a note. The Belgian chemicals company lowered its dividend for 2024 to 0.50 euro a share from 0.80 euro a year before and reset its policy. This means the company's policy of paying stable to rising dividends has been reset to a lower level and Umicore will stop interim payouts, the analyst says. Umicore's results benefited from higher-than-expected cost savings that helped the company offset severe headwinds in its battery-materials division, ING says. Shares fall 7.8%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0904 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index closed flat at 1591.60. The local market stayed in consolidation mode due to limited domestic catalysts and ongoing uncertainty over U.S. tariff policies, TA Securities analyst Stephen Soo said in a note. Soo pegs the KLCI's support at 1550 and resistance at 1605. Among decliners, CelcomDigi lost 8.5% as analysts raised concerns about its outlook. 99 Speed Mart Retail was 2.7% lower. Meanwhile, Genting Malaysia added 6.2% and Sunway Bhd. rose 1.7%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0856 GMT - Hermes International's latest results mean the Birkin bag maker joins the elite club of luxury companies smashing fourth-quarter forecasts, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. All divisions of the French luxury group contributed to the better-than-expected revenue performance, Bernstein says. The result of its leather goods-and-saddlery business shows the resilience and popularity of the brand among affluent customers, the analysts say. Hermes posted fourth-quarter revenue growth of more than 20% excluding currency changes in three markets--Europe excluding France, Japan and the Americas. The strong performance in Europe is consistent with the company's expectations and strength in the Americas has been shared by other luxury companies, according to Bernstein. In Japan, there is no sign of a slowdown, the analysts add. Shares rise 2.9%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0847 GMT - Safran's year-end results offered few surprises as its results were in line with consensus expectations, JPMorgan analysts say in a research note. The French aerospace-industry supplier increased its guidance for civil aircraft engine spare part sales for the year to high-single digit percentage growth from up from mid-single to high-single digit growth previously, a move the analysts say doesn't seem spectacular. Nevertheless, consensus expectations for this year may rise by around 1% after the company raised its adjusted earnings and cash guidance for the year. Shares trade 0.65% higher at 247.40 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0837 GMT - Heightened uncertainty over the trade tariffs U.S. President Trump could implement means investors need to ensure they have diverse portfolios and employ hedging strategies, says UBS Global Wealth Management's Mark Haefele. "While the delay in implementation limits immediate market impact, the risk of retaliation remains," the chief investment officer says. Within equities, capital preservation strategies could be necessary to prevent losses if stocks turn lower. UBS GWM favors high-grade and investment-grade bonds as a way to diversify portfolios as these offer "insulation against uncertainty." (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0820 GMT - Hong Kong shares closed higher, supported by healthcare and tech stocks. Investor sentiment remained positive on Chinese equities, thanks to the momentum boost from DeepSeek. Alibaba Health Information Technology surged 29%, Wuxi Biologics rose 12% and WuXi AppTec gained 10.0%. Tech stocks were also higher with investors watching for Chinese President Xi's meeting with top business executives next week, according to a media reports. Kuaishou Technology rose 7.65% and JD.com rose 7.6%. Among the few decliners, WH Group led the losses, falling 2.3% and Power Assets declining 1.2%. The benchmark Hang Seng Index ended 3.7% higher at 22620.33 and Hang Seng Tech Index rose 5.6%. Both indexes rose around 7% for the week. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0820 GMT - Segro's 2024 results show the industrial property market turned a corner in the second half, with property values increasing for the first time in several years, Quilter Cheviot analyst Oli Creasey says in a note. Although the increase was only 1.1%, the change in direction is an important datapoint, considering the cloud that U.K. real estate has been operating under since interest rates began increasing, Creasey says. Still, although property performance remains strong, earnings growth was muted due to a strong2023 comparison when Segro earned almost 90 million pounds from performance fees, he says. "Segro is invested in one of the more exciting property sectors, and the pipeline of European data center projects is particularly promising," Creasey adds. Shares are down 0.1% at 721.60 pence.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0807 GMT - NatWest's guidance for 2025 lack some punch, Hargreaves Lansdown says in a market comment after the British bank's results. The lender's expectations for this year align with consensus estimates. "The buzz around the sector and better-than-expected results in the final quarter mean investors will have been hoping to get a punchier outlook," analyst Matt Britzman writes, adding this might cause weakness in early exchanges. In the longer term, the decent set of results--which slightly beat expectations, closing a 2024 that saw a seismic positive sentiment shift--set up the bank for another good year. Shares open 1.4% lower at 431 pence, bringing its year to date gains to around 7%. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0750 GMT - Robinsons Retail Holdings' same-store sales growth is poised to accelerate in 2025, SB Equities' Lance Gabriel Soledad says in a research report. During its 4Q earnings call, the multi-format retailer stated that it's targeting SSSG of 2%-4% in 2025, compared to 2024's blended SSSG of 1.5%, the analyst notes. The Philippine company also aims for a gross profit margin increase of 20-30 basis points, along with net store additions of 130-170 this year. Moreover, the prospects of slowing inflation in the Philippines may drive a progressive rebound in the company's discretionary formats. The brokerage raises its target price to PHP57.50 from PHP50.22, partly reflecting the rollover of its earnings estimates, while maintaining an unchanged "add" rating. Shares are up 0.1% at PHP36.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0749 GMT - The U.K.'s FTSE 100 index is expected to open slightly higher, up 0.1%, according to IG, while Germany's DAX looks set to open 0.4% lower after hitting record highs recently. The recent rally in stocks has been helped by earnings data, says XTB's Kathleen Brooks in a note. Traders have largely shrugged off tariff threats for now, but the prospect of Trump announcing tariffs against European goods still looms. President Trump ordered federal agencies on Thursday to explore how to adjust U.S. tariffs to match of those of other countries. Brooks says these could take longer to plan. France's CAC 40 is expected to open down 0.1%, according to IG. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: Sichuan Hongda Completes Setting Up Chemical, Nonferrous Branches
data.description:
Sichuan Hongda completed the establishment of its phosphorus chemical and nonferrous branches, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

Sichuan Hongda received the business license for the Shifang Phosphorus Chemical Branch and Nonferrous Metals Branch from the local government of China's Shifang City, the Chinese chemical manufacturer said.

The company's shares closed 1% higher.

data.title: FX, stocks higher after Trump delays tariffs; rate decisions in focus
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump team devising plans for reciprocal tariffs
  • Analyst: FX markets think Trump posturing
  • Report: US VP threatening pressure on Russia
  • Monetary policy decisions due in Russia, Romania
  • MSCI EM FX up 0.3%, stocks advance 0.9%

Most emerging markets rose on Friday, as investors enjoyed a short reprieve when U.S. President Donald Trump did not immediately impose threatened reciprocal tariffs, and with interest rate decisions due in Russia and Romania.

Adding to a recent flood of tariff-related headlines, Trump tasked his economics team with devising plans for reciprocal tariffs on every country taxing U.S. imports on Thursday.

Analysts expect this process to leave room for negotiations, as with India, where it agreed to start talks for an early trade deal and resolve their standoff over tariffs, promising to buy more American energy and military equipment.

"The tariffs are due to come into effect in April, implying that the next few weeks could see intense negotiations with U.S. trading partners to avoid or reduce tariffs," said Mohit Kumar, chief European economist at Jefferies.

The dollar index dipped slightly, lifting MSCI's index tracking global EM currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) to its highest in more than two months and setting it for its best week in three.

"The lack of concern about the latest tariffs from Trump suggests that the FX market continues to think that he is posturing, and will tone down the tariffs at the last minute," said Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB.

The Romanian leu was flat ahead of a local monetary policy decision in which the central bank is widely expected to leave rates unchanged.

Russia's rouble fell 0.7% against the dollar, after U.S. Vice President JD Vance told a newspaper that the U.S. could hit Moscow with sanctions and potential military action if Russia does not agree to a peace deal with Ukraine that guarantees its long-term independence.

Vance, among a slew of other policymakers, was scheduled to speak at the Munich Security Conference.

The rouble gained for the last four sessions and was on track for its best week in six. An interest rate decision was due later on Friday.

The South African rand strengthened 0.5%, while its stocks jumped 1.2%. The index hit record highs in the last three sessions, and is approaching three weeks of gains.

Regional stocks were mixed on the day, with indexes in Romania up 0.7% and Poland down 0.3%. MSCI's stocks gauge CBOE:EFS was up 0.9%.

Emerging Europe currencies were mixed against the euro . Hungary's forint was set for its sixth consecutive week of declines, its longest losing streak since August 2021.

Data showed Polish inflation at 5.3% on an annual basis in January, above forecasts of 5%.

S&P is due to review Lebanon's rating on Friday, while Fitch will review the Czech Republic's.

Meanwhile, Senegal's finance minister said the country hopes for a new International Monetary Fund programme by June, as the government vowed to investigate alleged malfeasance after an audit found that former authorities misreported key data.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Buyout firms' equity-debt double act is creaking

** Chinese exporters brace for 'rat race' in shift away from US

** World Bank trims Thailand's 2025 GDP growth outlook

For TOP NEWS across emerging markets

data.title: Dutch benchmark hits two-week low on weather, storage hopes and peace talks
data.description:
The benchmark Dutch gas contract fell on Friday to its lowest in more than two weeks, weakening on forecasts of milder weather, talks over a loosening of European gas storage targets and ongoing U.S. efforts to end the war in Ukraine.

The benchmark front-month contract at the Dutch TTF hub (TRNLTTFMc1) was down 1.30 euros at 49.75 euros per megawatt hour (MWh), or $15.28/mmBtu, by 0913 GMT, LSEG data showed.

The contract fell earlier to 49.25 euros/MWh, the lowest level since January 29.

"The weather forecasts now agree that milder and windier conditions are on the way (probably by the middle of next week)," Energi Danmark analysts wrote.

The British weekend contract (TRGBNBPWE) was down 4 pence at 125 pence per therm while the Dutch day-ahead contract (TRNLTTFD1) shed 1.58 euros to 50.25 euros/MWh.

News of a potential start of talks to end the war in Ukraine and separate discussions on easing EU gas storage rules may have led some market participants to bet on higher prices to exit and take profit, consultancy Auxilione said in a market report.

"Whilst there is no actual outcome of either story as yet, this may limit how far down the market will move."

Germany supports less rigid storage targets because of worries over the cost of filling them in the current market environment, it said after a meeting between EU countries and the European Commission on Thursday.

EU rules stipulate storage sites must be 90% full by Nov. 1, with several interim targets along the way. EU gas storage sites have depleted faster this winter because of colder and less windy weather and are now about 47% full, data from Gas Infrastructure Europe shows.

Meanwhile, the U.S. continues to push for an end to the war in Ukraine, with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy expected to meet U.S. Vice President JD Vance in Munich on Friday.

In the European carbon market (CFI2Zc1), the benchmark contract was up 0.28 euros at 78.33 euros a metric ton.

data.title: Maxis Appoints Johan Idris as Chairman, Effective March 1
Hermes Executives Spoke During a Call After Results
Celcomdigi's Profit Tumbles in Q4 2024
data.description:
CelcomDigi's profit attributable to owners fell to 157.0 million ringgit in the fourth quarter of 2024, from 435.2 million ringgit a year earlier.

Basic earnings per share slid to 0.0134 ringgit from 0.0371 ringgit a year ago, according to a Thursday bursa filing.

Meanwhile, revenue inched up year on year to 3.28 billion ringgit from 3.27 billion ringgit, the Malaysian communications conglomerate and mobile service provider said.

The company declared a fourth interim tax exempt dividend of 0.037 ringgit per share for the financial year ended Dec. 31, 2024, which will be paid on March 27.

The company's shares closed nearly 9% lower on Friday.

data.title: Buchang Pharmaceuticals Subsidiary Completes Industrial, Commercial Registration
data.description:
Shandong Buchang Pharmaceuticals said subsidiary Changrui Biotechnology completed its industrial and commercial registration.

The subsidiary has obtained the business license reissued by the Chengdu High-tech Industrial Development Zone Market Supervision and Administration Bureau, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the pharmaceutical company rose 2% Friday.

data.title: Manazel Resumes Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Manazel (ADX:MANAZEL) recommenced trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange on Friday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started at 11:35 am local time on Friday and was lifted after the United Arab Emirates-based real estate company published the results of its meeting.

data.title: Market Chatter: CNGR Advanced Material in in Talks for Possible Hong Kong Listing
data.description:
CNGR Advanced Material is in talks with Huatai Securities and Morgan Stanley to seek help regarding its listing in Hong Kong, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The Shenzhen, China-listed battery component manufacturer could raise between $400 million and $500 million from the listing, the report said, citing the sources.

CNGR is still in talks regarding the details of the offering, Bloomberg said.

The company supplies batteries for Tesla, Samsung SDI , LG Chem , and Contemporary Amperex Technology , the report said.

CNGR did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Oculis Holding Prices Offering of 5 Million Shares
data.description:
Oculis Holding said Thursday it priced an underwritten offering of 5 million ordinary shares at $20 apiece, targeting gross proceeds of $100 million.

The company said the "oversubscribed" offering will likely close on or about Feb. 18. The new shares will bring the total number of registered shares to more than 53.9 million before closing.

Oculis intends to use the net proceeds to, among other things, advance and accelerate its clinical development pipeline, particularly its neuroprotective clinical candidate Privosegtor, or OCS-05.

data.title: Bayer Seeks Market Authorization in Japan for Heart Failure Treatment
data.description:
Bayer filed a marketing authorization application in Japan for its finerenone treatment for adult patients with chronic heart failure.

The application was filed with the Ministry of Health, Labour, and Welfare based on positive data from the phase 3 Finearts-HF study under the Moonraker phase 3 clinical program, according to a Friday release.

The pharmaceutical company added that applications for finerenone are also under review in the US, the European Union, and China.

data.title: COMMENT-Tariff war could be a game changer for the Viking cross
data.description:
There are already signs that the NOK/SEK cross might be headed higher but, as the world gets to grips with increasing protectionism, Norway's advantage over its neighbour in terms of export exposure might underpin further NOK gains.

The Viking cross has been in downtrend since March 2022 and, even after a near 15% depreciation in the NOK, there is room for NOK/SEK to go lower. However, in the shorter-term there might be scope for the NOK to reverse the more recent, since November 2024, 4.8% decline from 0.9999.

In recent weeks NOK/SEK has been capped, on a closing basis, by the 100-week moving average and the line, which is backed up by the weekly Ichimoku cloud top, could present a problem for the NOK.

The daily chart is showing potential for a Friday bullish engulfing candle. Engulfing signals can warn of a direction change and show up when a small real body (shaded area between the high and low) is completely engulfed by the following candle's real body.

The bullish daily action has led to a doji candle on the weekly chart, again a warning of a wind change or a period of consolidation.

Norway's crown still has work to do to get back onside but more favourable charts and Sweden's greater sensitivity to a trade war and global growth uncertainty could help underpin NOK/SEK.

For more click on

data.title: Nestle's organic sales in Slovenia rise 3.8% in 2024
data.description:
LJUBLJANA (Slovenia), February 14 (SeeNews) - Swiss food group Nestle said that its organic sales in Slovenia increased by 3.8% on the year in 2024.

"Last year we achieved organic growth thanks to very good results in the categories of coffee, food, pet food under the Purina brand, confectionery products and (food-service dedicated group) Nestle Professional," Laurentiu-Florin Dimitriu, Nestle general manager for Croatia, Slovenia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, said in a press release on Thursday.

Nestle's global sales totalled 91.4 billion Swiss francs (101 billion/97 billion euro) last year, down 1.8% compared to 2023. Its organic growth rate was 2.2%.

(1 euro = 0.946 Swiss francs)

data.title: Aram Group Resumes Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
ARAM Group (ADX:ARAM) recommenced trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange on Friday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started Thursday and was lifted after the United Arab Emirates-based real estate investor published the results of its meeting.

data.title: China's Bank Loans Hit Record High in January
data.description:
Chinese bank loans hit a record high in January, official data showed Friday, as banks heeded Beijing's call to provide more support to the economy.

New yuan loans stood at 5.13 trillion yuan, equivalent to $703.72 billion, in January, up from 990 billion yuan in December, according to data released by the People's Bank of China.

The result beat the 4.3 trillion yuan anticipated by economists in a Wall Street Journal survey, and surpassed the prior record of 4.92 trillion yuan for January 2024.

Total social financing, a broader metric for credit that includes nonbank financing, stood at 7.06 trillion yuan in January.

China's M2, the broadest measure of money supply, rose 7.0% in January from the prior year, slower than December's 7.3% growth and the 7.4% expected by economists.

Write to Singapore Editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Jiangsu Libert's Unit Bags Module Installation Project for 226 Million Yuan
data.description:
Jiangsu Libert's unit, Shanghai Libert Engineering, won the bid for the installation of large-scale industrial modules for 226.2 million yuan, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The Chinese industrial module manufacturer will construct and install 5BDA and 7BUG modules in a diesel engine plant within 1,480 calendar days, the filing said.

China General Nuclear Power Engineering is the tenderer of the project.

data.title: Montenegro's Jan customs revenue up 18% y/y
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), February 14 (SeeNews) - Montenegro's customs revenue rose 18% year-on-year to 87 million euro ($90 million) in the first month of 2025, the customs agency said.

Revenue from customs duty increased by an annual 19% to 3.75 million euro in January, while revenue from value added tax rose 11% to 52.6 million euro in the period under review, the customs agency said in a statement on Thursday.

Excise duty revenue grew by 31% on the year to 30.7 million euro in January.

In 2024, Montenegro's customs revenue rose 10% to 1.29 billion euro, with all segments registering annual growth.

($ = 0.962 euro)

data.title: Mazda to Strengthen Production and Sales Systems in Thailand
data.description:
TOKYO, Feb 14, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Mazda Motor Corporation (Mazda) and Mazda Sales (Thailand) Co., Ltd. (Mazda Thailand) held a press conference today in Bangkok, Thailand, and announced their plan to strengthen Auto Alliance (Thailand) Co., Ltd., Mazda's manufacturing base in Thailand, through investing 5 billion Thai baht and developing it as a manufacturing hub for new compact SUVs with an annual production capacity of 100,000 units by leveraging the strengths of the local supply chain that the company has cultivated in Thailand to date. This will reinforce the plant's role as an export base focused on Japan and the ASEAN market which is expected to grow steadily, as well as strengthen sales in the Thai market where Mazda has been present for the past 70 years.

From 2025 to 2027, they plan to introduce a total of five models: two battery EVs, one plug-in hybrid and two hybrids. In Thailand, where the transition to electrification is progressing, Mazda will expand its electrified product line to appeal to a wider range of customers.

One of the models is the "Mazda6e," the new battery-electric vehicle that will be produced by Changan Mazda Automobile Co., Ltd., a joint venture between Mazda and Chongqing Changan Automobile Co., Ltd. (CA) in China. The public unveiling of the "Mazda6e" for the Thai market is scheduled for 2025. It is the latest electric vehicle that combines Mazda's strengths in design, Jinba-ittai driving experience, and craftsmanship with electrification and smart-cabin technologies of CA. This model is the product of a longstanding partnership with CA, and will be sold in China, Europe and Thailand.

Mazda's President and CEO, Masahiro Moro, who addressed the press conference, said, "Mazda has built a strong business foundation in Thailand over the past 70 years with the support of many people, including the Thai government. Once again, I would like to express my deepest gratitude to everyone involved. We place great importance on building relationships of trust and empathy with our stakeholders in order to realize our Purpose, which is to "ENRICH LIFE-IN-MOTION FOR THOSE WE SERVE." Based on this purpose, we will introduce electrified products that are uniquely Mazda and meet the needs of customers in Thailand where electrification is progressing, and strengthen the role of our plant in Thailand to serve as a base for exporting compact vehicles mainly to Japan and the ASEAN market."

Mazda will continue to evolve "the Joy of Driving" based on its "Radically Human" value toward the year 2030, and will strive to deliver "the Joy of Living" by creating moving mobility experiences for customers in their daily lives.

Source: mazda
